title: Kidney stones - self-care
license_notes: Use as public patient-education source support. Avoid verbatim copying into primitives unless reuse terms are confirmed for the exact content.
supports: Kidney stone self-care may include drinking fluids, taking prescribed medicines, and straining urine when instructed.
A stone can block urine flow and cause severe pain.
Patients should contact a clinician for severe pain, fever and chills, vomiting, cloudy or foul-smelling urine, or burning with urination.
